messages.content:
I can’t execute commands or run tests from here. Below are concise, practical steps and example commands you can use to run “load test task 7” yourself — pick the snippet that matches your test framework and environment, or tell me which one you use and I’ll give the exact command.

Quick checks
- Ensure required services are up (databases, APIs) and any env vars set (e.g., .env).
- Install dependencies (pip install -r requirements.txt or npm ci) and/or docker-compose up -d if the project uses containers.
- Locate the test definition if you don’t know where it is:
  - grep -R "task 7\|task7\|Task 7" -n .
  - ls tests/load || find . -type f -path "*load*"

Common runners — example commands

- pytest (Python)
  - Run a single file: pytest tests/load/test_task_07.py -q
  - Run by marker/name: pytest -k "task7" -q
  - Add verbose and JUnit: pytest tests/load/test_task_07.py -q --junitxml=reports/task7.xml

- k6 (JavaScript/Go)
  - k6 run tests/load/task7.js
  - With output: k6 run --out json=reports/task7.json tests/load/task7.js

- locust (Python)
  - Headless example: locust -f locustfile_task7.py --headless -u 200 -r 10 -t 5m --csv=reports/task7
  - Adjust users (-u), spawn-rate (-r), and duration (-t) to match the task definition.

- Artillery (Node)
  - artillery run tests/load/task7.yml -o reports/task7-report.json

- JMeter
  - jmeter -n -t tests/load/task7.jmx -l reports/task7.jtl -j reports/task7.log

- npm script (if the repo exposes a script)
  - npm run load:7
  - or npm run load-test -- --task=7 (depends on how scripts parse args)

Collecting and interpreting results
- Look for summary lines that include RPS/requests, errors, latency percentiles (p50/p95/p99), and throughput.
- Save raw output (JSON/CSV/JTL) and generate an HTML report if supported (k6/Artillery/Locust have report tools).
- If errors spike (non-2xx responses or connection failures), inspect service logs and verify resource limits (CPU/memory).

Troubleshooting tips
- If tests fail immediately, check network/hostnames and any auth tokens.
- Increase timeouts and verify system under test can handle the load (consider ramp-up).
- If you need to run against a staging cluster, confirm its test data and isolation.

If you tell me which framework (pytest/k6/locust/artillery/JMeter/other), the repository path to the test, or paste the task definition, I’ll give the exact command string and recommended runtime flags. If you want me to run it here, I can’t execute commands — run the command locally and paste the output and I’ll help interpret failures or results.
